I'm going to travel next month.
She's going to learn the system.
Greg is going to visit Sao Paulo.
We're going to change the plan.
Tomorrow I'm going to send the report.
Next week I'm going to call the client.
Next month I'm going to visit Miami.
Next year I'm going to learn the new system.
Soon I'm going to speak on every call.
Hi, Paula. Good to see you. Are you ready for April?
Hi, Greg. Yes. What are we going to do first?
First, I'm going to send you the new system this week. Then you're going to try it with a small client.
OK. And are you going to visit Sao Paulo?
Yes. I'm going to fly to Sao Paulo on April tenth. I'm going to stay for two weeks.
Two weeks. Are we going to train the whole team?
We are. And you're going to help me. You know the team, and I don't.
I'm going to help, but I'm not going to translate everything. My team is going to learn too.
Good. That's a better plan. Sorry, are you going to be at the airport?
No, I'm not going to go to the airport. I'm going to meet you at the office.
Perfect. One more thing. After April, are you going to take the international accounts?
I don't know yet. But I'm going to say yes.
I'm going to learn the new system.
I'm not going to change jobs.
What are you going to do next?
Where are you going to be in one year?

Hi, Paula. David here. Before your call with Greg, I want to give you something, and it is a gift, because today the English is on your side for once. You are going to learn the future tense. I know that word scares you, but listen. In Portuguese you say it all day. Eu vou viajar. Eu vou aprender. Eu vou mudar. You already have it. In English it is the same thing, word for word. I am going to travel. I am going to learn. I am going to change. Vou is going to. That is the whole rule, and there is nothing new to fear. Now let me tell you my plans, because I have some. In April I am going to visit Sao Paulo. I am going to bring the new policy system, and I am going to train your whole team on it. We are going to work together for three months, you and me. And here is the part I want you to hear. I am not going to speak fast, and I am not going to use hard words. I am going to speak like a normal person, because that is what your English is ready for now. See you in April, Paula. Start making your plans.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. I want to talk to you about the future, your future, and not about grammar this time. I have watched you for a year now. In lesson one, you could not say your own job in English. Today you are going to take a call with the director in Miami, alone, and you are going to be fine. That is not small, Paula. That is a year of work. So here is my plan, and I am telling you first. Next month I am going to recommend you for the international desk. The large accounts, the calls in English, the clients abroad. I am not going to do it because your English is perfect, because it is not, and it does not need to be. I am going to do it because you ask when you do not understand, and because you never guess a number. Those are the things that matter here. Now I want one thing from you. Tonight, think about your own plans. Where are you going to be in one year? What are you going to learn? Do not tell me you are too old, or that it is too late, because I have watched you prove that wrong for twelve lessons. See you Monday, Paula. The future is yours.
